message:
Fix desktop nav centering with CSS grid, resolve layoutId collision

site-header.tsx:
- Switch desktop navbar inner layout from flex justify-between to a
  3-column CSS grid (grid-cols-3). The logo occupies column 1
  (justify-start), navigation links occupy column 2 (justify-center),
  and tools occupy column 3 (justify-end). This guarantees the nav
  links are perfectly horizontally centered in the header regardless
  of the logo or tools width, which flex justify-between cannot do
  when the left and right sections have different widths.
- Adjust inactive nav link color from slate-400 to slate-300 for
  better readability against the dark background.
- Tighten letter-spacing from 0.2em to 0.15em on nav links to reduce
  horizontal footprint and prevent wrapping at common viewport widths.
- Add border-transparent on the non-scrolled state to prevent a subtle
  1px layout shift when the border-green-500/20 class toggles on
  scroll (transparent border occupies the same space as colored border).

war-stories-map.tsx:
- Rename layoutId from "node-{title}" to "map-node-{title}" to prevent
  Framer Motion layout animation collisions. Multiple components on the
  war stories page used "node-" prefixed layoutIds, causing incorrect
  cross-component layout transitions when elements with matching IDs
  mounted/unmounted simultaneously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
